source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A tower is made up of bricks that are labelled with the numbers from 1 to 50 from bottom to top. Bob uses these bricks to build a new tower. Each time he takes the two topmost bricks off the old tower and places them down on top of the new tower without changing their order (see diagram). Which two bricks lie on top of each other when he is finished with the re-arrangement? <image1>

multi_step: question_text describes a repeated transformation: Bob removes the two topmost bricks each time and places them on a new tower
the operation preserves within-pair order, so solving requires tracking ordering constraints across many moves
the tower contains numbered bricks from 1 to 50, indicating nontrivial sequence/pattern reasoning rather than a single lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and answer_text is present
choices are provided, so the final result can be checked against a fixed option set
the rearrangement process is deterministic from the stated rules, making the claimed pair checkable

decision_reasons: question, answer, and 1 image are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong: image_dependency_score=0.82, multi_step_score=0.74, verifiability_score=0.94
the only noted risk is a limited caution that the diagram may be explanatory only, which is not sufficient to downgrade or reject
